hero.title: 3 Days in Shirdi.
hero.titleAccent: A Pilgrimage to Sai Baba's Sacred Town.
hero.intro: For millions, Shirdi is a place of deep faith. The town where Sai Baba lived and taught draws pilgrims from across the world. This gentle 3 day journey covers his shrine and the powerful Shani temple nearby.

overview.heading: 3 Days Shirdi Pilgrimage & Shani Shingnapur: The Full Picture
body: Shirdi is one of India's most visited pilgrimage towns, the home of the revered Sai Baba whose message of faith, charity and unity crossed every religious line. The Samadhi Mandir, where his remains rest, is the spiritual centre, and the daily aartis here are deeply moving for devotees and visitors alike.

This 3 day Shirdi pilgrimage package from Malaysia is arranged privately so your darshan and prayers happen on your own terms. Beyond the main shrine, you visit Dwarkamai, Chavadi and the powerful Shani Shingnapur temple, a village famous for having no doors on its houses, such is the faith in Lord Shani's protection.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Sai Baba Samadhi Mandir
items.body: The heart of Shirdi, where Sai Baba's samadhi rests beneath a beautiful marble statue. The morning Kakad Aarti and evening Dhoop Aarti are the spiritual high points of any visit.

items.title: Dwarkamai & Chavadi
items.body: The old mosque where Sai Baba spent much of his life, and the Chavadi where he rested on alternate nights. The sacred dhuni fire he lit still burns in Dwarkamai today.

items.title: Shani Shingnapur
items.body: A short drive away, this village temple to Lord Shani is famous nationwide. Legend holds that nobody locks their doors here, trusting in Shani's protection. A unique and powerful experience.

days.title: Arrive Shirdi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel near the temple complex. Evening free for your first darshan or aarti.

days.title: Shirdi Temples & Shani Shingnapur
days.description: A full day with your private vehicle covering Shirdi's sacred sites and a drive to Shani Shingnapur.

days.title: Final Darshan, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. A last darshan and prasad before your airport transfer.

whyBook.heading: Pilgrimage Trips, Handled With Care
whyBook.body: A pilgrimage should feel peaceful, not stressful. We arrange your stays close to the temple, time your visits to avoid the worst queues where possible, and handle all the logistics so you can focus on your darshan. Dhesu has arranged India pilgrimages since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Shirdi Pilgrimage Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
Shirdi is one of the most meaningful pilgrimages in India, and going with a private arrangement makes the experience far smoother. Hotels near the temple, a vehicle for the Shani Shingnapur leg, and a team that understands the rhythm of darshan and aarti.

The town is welcoming and well set up for pilgrims, with the Sai Baba Sansthan Trust managing the main shrine. Our package handles the practical side so your focus stays on the spiritual one.

sections.heading: Combining Shirdi with Nashik and Shani Shingnapur
sections.body: Many devotees extend their Shirdi trip to include Nashik, a holy city on the Godavari river, and the Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga. Our 4 day Shirdi, Nashik and Shani Shingnapur package brings these together for a fuller Maharashtra pilgrimage.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Shirdi Airport or Pune International Airport (SAG / PNQ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Shirdi?
items.answer: Shirdi is open year-round. November to February offers the most comfortable weather. Festivals like Ram Navami, Guru Purnima and Vijayadashami draw the largest crowds, so book early if you wish to visit during these.

items.question: How long does darshan take at the Samadhi Mandir?
items.answer: It varies greatly with the crowd, from under an hour to several hours during festivals. We arrange stays close to the temple and advise on quieter timings. VIP and special darshan options are sometimes available.

items.question: Is vegetarian and prasad food available?
items.answer: Yes. Shirdi is largely vegetarian, and the Sai Baba Sansthan runs a prasadalaya serving simple meals to thousands of pilgrims daily. Your hotel and nearby restaurants also serve vegetarian food.
